title: HPKE, Hybrid Encryption, and Envelope Systems · Mastery Check
summary: A release board compares near-miss decisions for hpke, hybrid encryption, and envelope systems and accepts only bounded behavior, causal changed-case evidence, and an explicit native, entropy, interoperability, side-channel, compliance, or production transfer gate. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for multi-recipient envelope encryption format. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Combine public-key key establishment with symmetric AEAD for bounded scalable payload encryption and explicit recipient identity.
Use Go HPKE suites and modes with exact KEM, KDF, AEAD, info, associated data, key encoding, and one-shot or context lifecycle.
Generate per-object data keys, wrap them under recipient or key-encryption keys, authenticate metadata, and separate data from key custody.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Cryptography and Go Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Crypto Go Rewrap Without Reencrypt
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A quiz cryptographic engineering team handles case 4953 during a backup restoration drill. Build deterministic Go evidence to bind an HPKE or hybrid envelope to recipient, object, suite, payload, and audited rewrap state; keep browser execution deterministic and move production entropy, native timing, non-allowlisted crypto, network, HSM, and compliance claims to authorized transfer gates; then remove one transfer gate and state exactly which production claim is no longer supported. Browser code uses pure state models or the narrow side-effect-free crypto allowlist with fixed fixtures. Production entropy and keys, secret custody, native timing and side channels, networks, TLS, HSM/KMS, certificate systems, FIPS or compliance state, load, faults, restore, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Rotate recipient or KEK protection by authenticated rewrapping while preserving payload identity, audit, rollback, and revocation limits.

The visible result looks correct, but its invariant or value contract is unverified. What must happen next?

Evidence target: Cryptography and Go Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Crypto Go Rewrap Without Reencrypt.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for rotate recipient or kek protection by authenticated rewrapping while preserving payload identity, audit, rollback, and revocation limits. For Cryptography and Go Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Crypto Go Rewrap Without Reencrypt, keep Crypto Go Rewrap Without Reencrypt connected to executable Go behavior evidence.
